description: Event-driven automation engine that triggers actions based on record lifecycle events, field changes, time-based schedules, incoming messages, and external webhooks with condition filtering.

actors.name: Automation Admin
actors.description: Creates and manages automation rules, conditions, and actions

actors.name: Automation Engine
actors.description: Monitors events, evaluates conditions, executes actions

message_triggers_require_mail_model.description: Message-received and message-sent triggers are only valid on record types that support messaging/threading.

on_change_only_code_actions.description: The on_change trigger (UI field change) can only execute code-type actions, not emails, SMS, or record creation.

trigger_field_tracking.description: When specific trigger fields are set, the rule only fires if at least one of those fields actually changed in the update operation.

before_after_domain_transition.description: Pre-filter and post-filter conditions together define a state transition: record must have matched the before-filter and must now match the after-filter for the rule to fire.

webhook_uuid_immutable.description: Webhook endpoint identifiers cannot be changed after creation to ensure external integrations remain stable.

time_trigger_cron_scheduling.description: Time-based rules are evaluated via periodic scheduled jobs. The delay is calculated from the specified date field on each record.

no_recursive_automation.description: Actions executed by an automation rule do not trigger other automation rules to prevent infinite loops (guard flag set).

given: a new record is created matching the target model
trigger type is on_create or on_create_or_write
the new record matches the filter condition (if set)

then.description: Execute configured actions (email, field update, code, etc.)

rule_triggered_on_create.result: Actions executed automatically on the new record

given: one of the watched trigger fields changed
record matched the before-filter BEFORE the change
record matches the after-filter AFTER the change

then.description: Execute configured actions on the changed record

rule_triggered_on_field_change.result: Actions executed based on the specific field transition

given: record's stage/state/priority/tag/user field is set to the trigger value
rule_triggered_on_stage_set.result: Actions fire when the specific value is reached

given: records exist where the date field + delay has passed
those records match the filter condition

then.description: Execute actions on all matching records in batch

rule_triggered_on_time.result: Time-delayed actions execute on all qualifying records

given: external system sends HTTP request to the webhook endpoint
webhook UUID matches an active rule

then.description: Parse webhook payload and execute code action with payload available as context variable

given: a message is received on (or sent from) a record
trigger type is on_message_received or on_message_sent

then.description: Execute actions with message content available in context

given: rule fires and action type is send_email

then.description: Email sent using configured template with record data merged

action_sends_email.result: Automated email sent to the record's contacts

given: rule fires and action type is set_field

then.description: Field value set to configured static or computed value

given: trigger event occurs but record does not match filter conditions

errors.message: The target record type does not exist or is not accessible.
Message triggers are only available for record types that support messaging.
An error occurred while executing the automation code action.
No active automation rule found for this webhook endpoint.

events.name: automation.rule.fired
events.description: An automation rule was triggered and its actions executed

events.name: automation.rule.scheduled_batch
events.description: Time-based rule executed on a batch of records

events.name: automation.webhook.received
events.description: External webhook triggered an automation rule

related.reason: Automate actions on sales orders (e.g., auto-assign team on creation)
Automate expense workflow steps (e.g., auto-approve under threshold)
